Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd (in liquidation)

Facts.Text: Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd, a company incorporated in Singapore, was placed in liquidation on 2 July 2021.

On 25 February 2020, Mr Law wrote to Ascendas Hospitality Real Estate Investment Trust and New Park Property Limited to request a revision of the rent and other charges payable under the Park Hotel Clarke Quay and Grand Park Orchard leases, citing declining hotel occupancy rates and higher costs to implement safety measures due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

On 21 April 2020, Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd applied under the COVID-19 (Temporary Measures) Act 2020 for temporary relief from their contractual obligations under their respective leases.

Temporary relief under the COVID-19 (Temporary Measures) Act 2020 was granted to Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd until 19 November 2020, after which they were required to resume full payment of rent and other charges, pay monthly instalments for arrears under the Statutory Repayment Scheme, and top up their security deposits.

According to draft financial statements for the financial year ending 31 December 2020, Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd had net current liabilities of S$18,964,276 and net total liabilities of S$4,654,826, and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd had net current liabilities of S$15,666,721 and net total liabilities of S$11,569,730.

Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd furnished Ascendas Hospitality Real Estate Investment Trust and New Park Property Limited each a guarantee and indemnity to secure the liabilities of Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd under their respective leases.

As a result of the COVID-1 outbreak, Singapore shut its borders on 23 March 2020. Visitor arrivals fell sharply and dining-in was prohibited during the Circuit Breaker measures, affecting the Park Hotel Group.

On 11 September 2020, Grand Park OR Pte Ltd transferred S$1,000,000 to Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's HSBC account, from which S$3,419,682.46 was transferred to the Laws' UBS Account on the same day.

Between 9 December 2020 and 4 January 2021, Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd transferred a total of S$4,000,000 to Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's UOB account, resulting in a balance of S$4,413,505.21, which was transferred on 8 January 2021 from that UOB account to the Laws' UBS Account.

On 24 December 2020, Grand Park OR Pte Ltd transferred S$1,998,681.69 to Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's HSBC account, and on 9 January 2021 S$1,968,604.46 was transferred from that HSBC account to the Laws' UBS Account.

On 9 December 2020, Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd again applied for temporary relief under the COVID-19 (Temporary Measures) Act 2020 on the basis that their respective leases were tourism-related contracts, despite advice from Tan Kok Quan Partnership that the applications were unlikely to succeed.

On 8 March 2021, Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd entered into an Asset and Share Transfer Agreement with Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd, a Framework Agreement with Good Movement Holdings Limited, and a Business Transfer Agreement with SG Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd.
On 23 March 2021, Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd entered into a Trademark Assignment Agreement with Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd.
On 1 March 2021, before the Framework Agreement was signed, Mr Law procured PHMPL to transfer its shares in Yan Pte Ltd to GMHL.
On or around 3 March 2021, Mr Law procured PHMPL to declare a S$22m Dividend but back-dated this to 1 September 2020.

On 12 March 2021, Mr Law caused PHMPL to draw down S$5,000,000 from its UOB loan facility, which was paid into PHMPL's UOB account, such that it had a credit of S$6,700,984.09; on the same day, S$6,698,130.81 was transferred from PHMPL's UOB account to the Laws' UBS Account.
On 6 April 2021, Mr Law approved the transfer of S$1,054,089.06 from PHMPL's HSBC account to his personal UOB account.
On 5 April 2021, Mr Law procured PHMPL to declare a S$5.9m Dividend but back-dated this to 23 March 2021.

In March 2021, Mr Law announced to PHMPL's employees that there would be a restructuring and new entities would be formed, and that all employees would be transferred to the new entity effectively in April, with no change in employment arrangements apart from the legal employer.

Mr Ng's employment with PHMPL officially ceased on 31 March 2021 and his employment with PHGM began on 1 April 2021 on identical terms; however, his letter of cessation of employment with PHMPL and letter of employment with PHGM were only prepared and signed in May 2021.

On 31 March 2021, New Park Property Limited sent letters of demand to Grand Park OR Pte Ltd and Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd, and on 5 April 2021 PHMPL stated that it would not be making payment of the sums claimed by NPP in respect of the GPOR Lease on the grounds of frustration.

On 4 March 2021, Mr Law and Ms Tang discussed by e-mail whether adding a line to an amendment agreement with ART would help the frustration case later and whether they needed to negotiate with GPOR's landlord to buy time.
On 1 March 2021, Ms Tang informed Mr Law that PHMPL's disposal of Yan Pte Ltd was a substantial disposal required to be lodged with ACRA, and Mr Law replied '[l]ater the better'.
On 2 March 2021, Mr Law instructed Ms Tang to lodge the change of directors of PHCQ and GPOR only after all the Contracts had been transferred out of PHMPL.
On 16 March 2021, Mr Law appointed Mr Lim, the general manager of both Park Hotel Clarke Quay and Grand Park Orchard hotels, to replace him as director of PHCQ and GPOR.

Mr Law instructed for the ownership of PHGM to be transferred to GMHL, and he instructed for the ownership of Yan Pte Ltd to be transferred to GMHL and for the SIH business to be transferred to SIOHPL.
On 4 March 2021, Ms Tang prepared and sent the computation for the S$200,000 consideration under the BTA to Mr Law, and he approved it within minutes.
On 3 March 2021, after Mr Law had signed the director's resolution declaring the S$22m Dividend, Ms Tang wrote to Mr Ng regarding the clearing of balances.

On 10 August 2017, Ms Tang sent an email to Mr Law outlining a proposal for a group restructuring that envisaged a single ultimate holding company and PHMPL remaining as the intermediate holding company for the hotel management business line.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the transfers of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's assets, businesses, and cash to companies owned or controlled by Mr Law, and related internal accounting entries and dividends, resulted in a depletion of assets available to meet liabilities, including exposure under corporate guarantees for subsidiary lease obligations.
Party_Details.Rule: Under company and insolvency law, transactions such as asset transfers, dividends, and set-offs may be set aside or give rise to liability if they occur when a company is unable to pay its debts as they fall due or becomes unable to do so as a result, and if they involve dispositions at an undervalue or preferential treatment of certain creditors or related parties compared to other creditors.
Party_Details.Application: Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd operated hotel management, food and beverage, and training businesses and held hotel management agreements, trademarks, and shares in subsidiaries. Its subsidiaries Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd stopped paying rent in 2020 and remained in arrears after temporary statutory relief expired. After a February 2021 demand from a landlord, Mr Law and Ms Tang proceeded with a restructuring. Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd entered into agreements to transfer hotel management contracts, trademarks, and subsidiary shares to entities owned or controlled by Mr Law. During this period, cash was routed through Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd to an account held by Mr Law and Ms Tan, and the company recorded large interim dividends used to offset amounts owing by Mr Law and his related entities. Employees and operational infrastructure were moved from the company while it remained exposed under corporate guarantees.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judge found that by December 2020, and at the latest by 20 February 2021 when New Park Property Limited issued a demand, Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd was unable to pay its debts as they fell due once proper allowance was made for its exposure under corporate guarantees and impairment of receivables. The court concluded that Mr Law orchestrated a restructuring to move all viable, revenue-generating assets and businesses from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd to companies he owned or controlled, leaving the company as a shell with substantial liabilities. The judge relied on contemporaneous emails to find that Mr Law and Ms Tang acted with urgency after the demand, discussing minimising the risk of challenge and seeking to shield the new structure from third-party claims. The court found that the Asset and Share Transfer Agreement, Framework Agreement, Business Transfer Agreement, and Trademark Assignment Agreement were not negotiated at arm's length, that Mr Law effectively decided the consideration on both sides, and that the consideration was significantly below the true value of the transferred assets. The judgment characterises the S$22 million and S$5.9 million dividends and the journal entries as devices to eliminate receivables owed by Mr Law and his entities, noting that these entries were backdated and created after the company was insolvent. The judge concluded that the asset transfers, dividends, cash extractions, and set-offs were transactions at an undervalue and unfair preferences, and that Mr Law breached his duties as director. The court further found that the defendant companies, being owned or controlled by Mr Law and benefiting from the transfers, are liable for accessory claims such as dishonest assistance, knowing receipt, and conspiracy.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Aw Eng Hai

Facts.Text: Aw Eng Hai is one of the liquidators of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd.
Aw Eng Hai is a licensed liquidator and chartered accountant.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the financial analysis conducted by the liquidator showed that Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd was unable to pay its debts as they fell due before the impugned transactions.
Party_Details.Rule: In assessing a company's solvency under the cash flow test, a liquidator may adjust the balance sheet to reflect realistic recoverability of receivables and contingent liabilities that are likely to be demanded, including obligations under guarantees.
Party_Details.Application: As liquidator, Aw Eng Hai examined the company's draft financial statements for the year ending 31 December 2020 and identified that receivables from subsidiaries were unlikely to be recoverable given their net liability positions and rental arrears. He also considered that the company had issued corporate guarantees and that demands under these guarantees were likely. By adjusting the balance sheet to impair receivables and recognise expected liabilities under the guarantees, he concluded that the company's current liabilities exceeded its current assets and that it could not meet its debts as they fell due by the end of 2020 or by February 2021.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court accepts the liquidator's methodology of adjusting Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's balance sheet to reflect the practical unrecoverability of receivables from insolvent subsidiaries and the realistic exposure under corporate guarantees. The judge notes that the subsidiaries had substantial net current and total liabilities and had ceased paying rent. The judgment endorses the liquidator's view that the company's recorded current assets were overstated and its current liabilities understated, and that once corrected, the company failed the cash flow test by December 2020. This analysis supports the finding that the company was insolvent when the asset transfers, dividends, and set-offs occurred, satisfying the statutory requirements for transactions at an undervalue and unfair preferences.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Kon Yin Tong

Facts.Text: Kon Yin Tong is one of the liquidators of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, as a joint liquidator, Kon Yin Tong participates in the administration and recovery actions arising from pre-liquidation transactions affecting the company's asset base.
Party_Details.Rule: Joint liquidators appointed upon a company's winding up are responsible for realising assets and pursuing claims to recover value lost through pre-liquidation transactions that may be set aside under insolvency and company law.
Party_Details.Application: Upon the winding up of the company on 2 July 2021, Kon Yin Tong assumed responsibility for investigating the company's affairs, including asset transfers, dividends, and set-offs executed before liquidation, and for bringing proceedings to recover value for creditors.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judgment identifies Kon Yin Tong as a co-liquidator of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd but does not attribute separate conduct or fault to him. His role is treated as part of the liquidators' collective function in bringing the action. The court's liability findings are directed at Mr Law and the defendant companies, not at the liquidators, and the reference to Kon Yin Tong serves to establish standing to pursue the claims and to implement the court's orders for recovery.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Law Ching Hung

Facts.Text: From at least 2017 until March 2021, Law Ching Hung was the sole shareholder and director of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd.
During the material period, Law Ching Hung was the sole shareholder and director of Good Movement Holdings Limited.
During the material period, Law Ching Hung was a director of Park Hotel CQ Pte Ltd and Grand Park OR Pte Ltd until 16 March 2021.
During the material period, Law Ching Hung was married to Ms Tan Shin Hui.

On 25 February 2020, Law Ching Hung wrote to Ascendas Hospitality Real Estate Investment Trust and New Park Property Limited requesting a revision of rent and other charges under the Park Hotel Clarke Quay and Grand Park Orchard leases, referring to declining occupancy and increased safety costs.

On 6 March 2020, in correspondence with Tan Kok Quan Partnership, Law Ching Hung referred to a corporate guarantee by Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd and stated that the group could not afford to go into default under the leases.
On 21 April 2020, in applications under the COVID-19 (Temporary Measures) Act 2020, Law Ching Hung stated that the subsidiaries were unable to pay rent from March and April 2020 respectively.

On 9 December 2020, despite advice that further applications were unlikely to succeed, Mr Law caused PHCQ and GPOR to apply again for temporary relief under the COVID-19 (Temporary Measures) Act 2020.

On 11 September 2020, after Grand Park OR Pte Ltd transferred S$1,000,000 to the company's HSBC account, S$3,419,682.46 was transferred from that account to a UBS account held jointly by Law Ching Hung and Ms Tan.
On 8 January 2021, S$4,413,505.21 was transferred from the company's UOB account to the Laws' UBS Account.
On 9 January 2021, S$1,968,604.46 was transferred from the company's HSBC account to the Laws' UBS Account.

On 23 February 2021, Mr Law appointed lawyers to prepare agreements to dispose of the company's assets and informed them of the need to ensure the restructuring exercise would shield the new setup from any third-party claims.
On 1 March 2021, Law Ching Hung signed a share transfer form to transfer the company's shares in Yan Pte Ltd to Good Movement Holdings Limited.
On 2 March 2021, Law Ching Hung selected which assets and businesses would be transferred to companies owned by Good Movement Holdings Limited, emphasising trademarks and hotel management contracts.
On 2 March 2021, Mr Law instructed that PHGM's ownership be transferred to GMHL and that Ms Tan replace him as director of PHGM.
On 3 March 2021, Mr Law approved a computation that set the consideration of S$2,700,000 for the transfer of hotel management contracts.
On or around 3 March 2021, Mr Law procured the company to declare a S$22m Dividend but back-dated this to 1 September 2020.
On 5 March 2021, Mr Law questioned the naming of the 'restructuring exercise' and suggested it be framed as an arm's length sale and purchase.
On 6 March 2021, Mr Law described the restructuring as 'more like a Sale and Purchase between the various entities'.
On 12 March 2021, Mr Law caused the company to draw down S$5,000,000 and transfer S$6,698,130.81 from the company's UOB account to the Laws' UBS Account.
On 16 March 2021, Mr Law appointed Mr Lim as director of the subsidiaries while Mr Law continued to manage both companies.
On 23 March 2021, the company agreed to assign 135 trademarks to PHGM for the consideration of S$1.
On 5 April 2021, Mr Law instructed Ms Tang to clear his director's account and pay the balance sum to him.
On 6 April 2021, Mr Law approved the transfer of S$1,054,089.06 from the company's HSBC account to his personal account.
In April and May 2021, Mr Law engaged in discussions with a landlord about repayment plans while the company had already transferred its hotel management business and other assets.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Law Ching Hung's control over the company and related entities, his decisions to transfer assets and businesses to companies he owned or controlled, and his directions regarding cash movements resulted in the removal of value from the company while it faced significant lease-related liabilities.
Party_Details.Rule: Company directors who control both a company and its counterparties must ensure that transactions are conducted on proper terms, particularly when the company is or may become unable to pay its debts. Transactions that move value to related parties at inadequate consideration may be treated as transactions at an undervalue or unfair preferences.
Party_Details.Application: As sole shareholder and director, Law Ching Hung controlled the key entities in the group. After the subsidiaries ceased paying rent and statutory relief expired, he initiated a restructuring to shield the new structure from third-party claims. He approved consideration figures based on internal computations without independent valuation. During the same period, cash was transferred from the company to a UBS account held jointly by him and his spouse. He signed resolutions declaring large interim dividends and used these and journal entries to clear his director's loan account and related-party balances.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court finds that Law Ching Hung was the central decision-maker in the group and that he used his control to prioritise his own interests over those of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd and its creditors. The judge concludes that he breached his duties as director by orchestrating a restructuring that transferred all viable assets out of the company at a time when it was insolvent, and by causing the company to enter into transactions at an undervalue and to give unfair preferences. The judgment emphasises that the transfer agreements were not negotiated at arm's length and that consideration was artificially depressed. The court finds that large interim dividends and journal entries were backdated and used to eliminate receivables owed by Law Ching Hung and his entities. The judge rejects explanations that backdating was routine and concludes that they were intended to hinder or delay creditor claims.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd is a company incorporated in Singapore in 2017 and was formerly known as Park Baobab Pte Ltd.

During the material period, Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd's sole director was Ms Tan Shin Hui, and it was owned by Good Movement Holdings Limited, which was wholly owned and controlled by Law Ching Hung.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd received hotel management contracts, trademarks, and operational staff from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd on terms that removed value from the transferor while both entities were under common control.
Party_Details.Rule: A company that receives assets from another company at a time when the transferor is insolvent, and where the recipient is owned or controlled by the same person who controls the transferor, may be subject to recovery actions if the transfers are at an undervalue.
Party_Details.Application: On 8 March 2021, Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd entered into an Asset and Share Transfer Agreement with Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd to acquire multiple hotel management agreements, trademarks, and business infrastructure for a stated consideration of S$2,700,000. On 23 March 2021, it entered into a Trademark Assignment Agreement to acquire 135 trademarks for S$1. The consideration was set by internal computations without independent valuation. From 1 April 2021, the company employed staff previously employed by the transferor and used the transferred infrastructure to continue the hotel management business.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court finds that Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd was effectively a recipient of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd's hotel management business and related assets under transactions orchestrated by Law Ching Hung when the transferor was insolvent. The judge concludes that the consideration paid was significantly below the true value of the assets and that the company did not assume corresponding liabilities. The court holds that Park Hotel Group Management Pte Ltd is liable for knowing receipt of assets transferred in breach of duty and for dishonest assistance, given its ownership structure and the involvement of its director in implementing the transfers. The company is also found to have participated in an unlawful means conspiracy to strip the transferor of its assets.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Good Movement Holdings Limited

Facts.Text: Under the Framework Agreement dated 8 March 2021, the company agreed to transfer to Good Movement Holdings Limited the entire issued share capital of Yan Pte Ltd for S$500,000 and the remaining 1% of Park Hotel Maldives for US$400.
On 1 March 2021, Mr Law procured the company to transfer its shares in Yan Pte Ltd to Good Movement Holdings Limited before the Framework Agreement had been signed.
On 3 March 2021, Yan Pte Ltd declared a dividend that had the effect of reducing its net asset value and the resulting consideration under the Framework Agreement to exactly S$500,000.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Good Movement Holdings Limited, owned and controlled by the same individual who controlled Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd, received shares and benefited from loans and asset transfers on terms that reduced the transferor's asset base while it faced significant liabilities.
Party_Details.Rule: A holding company that receives shares or benefits from loans from an insolvent company controlled by the same individual may be subject to recovery actions where the transfers are at an undervalue or constitute preferences, particularly when internal accounting entries are used to clear balances.
Party_Details.Application: Good Movement Holdings Limited was wholly owned and directed by Law Ching Hung. On 11 September 2020, S$3,419,682.46 transferred from the company to a UBS account held by Law Ching Hung and his spouse was recorded as a loan to Good Movement Holdings Limited. On 3 March 2021, Yan Pte Ltd declared a dividend that reduced its net asset value, and the Framework Agreement subsequently set the consideration for the Yan Pte Ltd shares at a matching amount. Internal accounting entries in the company's ledgers later used dividends and journal entries to clear amounts recorded as owing by Good Movement Holdings Limited without corresponding cash settlement.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court finds that Good Movement Holdings Limited was a key vehicle through which Law Ching Hung moved value out of Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd. The judgment notes that a substantial cash transfer was recorded as a loan to Good Movement Holdings Limited and that this receivable was later eliminated through the use of backdated dividends and set-offs. The court concludes that the acquisition of Yan Pte Ltd occurred at an undervalue, given the way net asset value was reduced and the absence of independent valuation. The judge holds that Good Movement Holdings Limited is liable for knowing receipt and dishonest assistance in relation to the breaches of duty by Law Ching Hung and for participation in an unlawful means conspiracy.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore on 4 March 2021 and was owned by Good Movement Holdings Limited.

On 8 March 2021, Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd entered into a Business Transfer Agreement to acquire the Singapore Institute of Hospitality business and related assets for a stated consideration of S$200,000.
On 4 March 2021, a computation for the S$200,000 consideration under the Business Transfer Agreement was approved within minutes of receipt.
On or around 8 March 2021, the Business Transfer Agreement was signed on behalf of Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd received a business and related assets from Park Hotel Management Pte Ltd for a consideration determined internally without independent valuation, at a time when the transferor faced significant liabilities.
Party_Details.Rule: A newly incorporated company that acquires assets from an insolvent company controlled by the same individual, for consideration set by internal computations without independent valuation, may be subject to recovery actions if the transfer is at an undervalue.
Party_Details.Application: Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd was incorporated shortly before the transfer and was owned by Good Movement Holdings Limited. Its director had previously managed the transferred business within the transferor company. A computation valuing the business at S$200,000 was approved by Law Ching Hung based on conservative assumptions. The executed agreement did not include a clause transferring liabilities to the recipient company, after Law Ching Hung instructed its removal. The agreement was signed without negotiation of the consideration or other key terms.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court finds that Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd was created as part of the restructuring to receive the Singapore Institute of Hospitality business. The judgment notes that the S$200,000 consideration was based on a computation that used conservative assumptions and ignored more optimistic forecasts. The court concludes that the valuation was artificially low and that the Business Transfer Agreement was at an undervalue. The judge finds that Sg. Inst. Of Hospitality Pte Ltd is liable for knowing receipt and dishonest assistance in relation to the breaches of duty by Law Ching Hung and for participation in an unlawful means conspiracy.
